relatability_triggers: Lead type: Concrete example cascade with dollar proof

headline.primary: How I turned a simple idea into a $100-Million Business
headline.subheadline: My simple secret will work for you if you are at the bottom of your company... or at the top... or anywhere in-between.

schema_analysis.expectation_violated: Claims the secret works for ANYONE at ANY level - not position-dependent tactics
schema_analysis.whitespace_territory: Universal career leverage principle rather than role-specific tactics

lead.type: Concrete example cascade with dollar proof
lead.full_text:
Opens with three specific examples:

'Would you pay $10 for each idea? I did. To a clerk whose question saved my company hundreds of thousands of dollars on postage every year.'

'Would you pay $2 for one of them? I did. To a young lady who spotted a way to put $10,000 back in our pocket in one order with a new vendor.'

'Would you pay nothing for one of them? I did. To a supplier who found how to simplify what he was doing for us, and passed the savings on without even being asked.'

Then revelation: 'These people have made small fortunes in the larger and larger profitsharings... Now wonder each of them gives me an average of 200 ideas a year!'
lead.opening_mechanism: Three ascending examples with dollar specificity
lead.curiosity_creation: How does paying small amounts for ideas create massive business value?

big_idea.statement: I-Power: Shared idea power where you pay people for ideas and share the success those ideas create - generating an average of 200 ideas per person per year.
big_idea.supporting_idea: It takes less than an hour or two to set up, costs nothing to learn, and creates exponential value for everyone involved.

mechanism.name: I-Power (Shared Idea Power)
mechanism.primary_mechanism: Pay people immediately for their ideas (even small amounts like $2, $10), share profits generated from ideas, creating idea-flow culture

mechanism_reveal_architecture.reveal_timing: Mechanism revealed through three concrete payment examples
mechanism_reveal_architecture.proof_to_mechanism_ratio: Equal proof and mechanism - examples ARE the mechanism

promise.primary_promise: Build an idea-flow engine that generates 200 ideas per person per year, creating exponential business growth and career advancement
promise.promise_specificity: 200 ideas per person per year, works at ANY organizational level
promise.timeframe: Within minutes of picking up the book, within an hour or two to set up

believability_factors: Specific dollar examples ($10, $2, $0 investments → massive returns)
Works for clerk, young lady, supplier - any level

strongest_proof_elements.text: $10 payment to clerk saved hundreds of thousands on postage yearly
$2 payment to young lady spotted $10,000 savings with vendor
'I-POWER is the best business book I've ever read... and I've read them all.' - Robert Half
'I-POWER produced an overpowering orgasm of useful ideas for us.' - Eric Solberg, Executive Director

proof_inventory.proof_density: High - multiple testimonials and specific dollar ROI examples

story_beats: Clerk asks question → saves hundreds of thousands → gets $10 + profit sharing
Young lady spots opportunity → saves $10,000 → gets $2 + profit sharing
Supplier simplifies process → passes savings on → gets nothing upfront but larger orders
All three make 'small fortunes' through larger profit-sharing
Each gives average 200 ideas per year
System builds everyone's future while building yours
story_elements.emotional_progression: Skepticism (tiny payments?) → Recognition (massive ROI) → Aspiration (I want this idea-flow)

metadata.author: Martin Edelston
metadata.target_audience: Business professionals at all levels seeking advancement

fssit_candidates.statement: Can I teach it to you in a few minutes. It costs you nothing to learn.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Explains why complex business strategies failed - this is SIMPLE and FREE to implement

fssit_candidates.statement: Am I taking advantage of these people? Not at all! I'm building their futures, at the same time that I'm building my own.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Addresses guilt about 'using' people's ideas - reframes as mutual wealth creation

fssit_candidates.statement: Now wonder each of them gives me an average of 200 ideas a year! And you can have this same kind of idea-flow coming to you, within minutes after you pick up this book!
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Explains why innovation felt hard - you were generating ideas alone instead of systematically harvesting 200 per person

rsf_analysis.fssit_placement_pattern: FSSITs address objections (cost, ethics, complexity) and amplify opportunity

schema_analysis.expectation_violated: Massive business growth comes from TINY idea payments to ANYONE at ANY level
schema_analysis.whitespace_territory: Micro-payments for ideas with profit-sharing rather than incentive programs or innovation initiatives

narrative_reorganization.domain_scope: Business management, career advancement, organizational innovation
narrative_reorganization.self_blame_removal: You haven't failed because you lack ideas or vision. Everyone around you has 200 ideas per year going unharvested.
narrative_reorganization.coherence_creation: All those expensive consultants, innovation workshops, and strategic initiatives that produced minimal results - they were complex and expensive. This is simple and free to learn.
narrative_reorganization.new_self_concept: Not someone who must generate ideas alone, but an orchestrator who unlocks collective genius through immediate micro-rewards and profit-sharing

temporal_analysis.cultural_moment_reference: Reference to '1990s' as success-engine but timeless principles

source_file: How I turned a simple idea.md

three_part_structure.what_they_think: Business growth requires expensive consultants, complex innovation programs, or visionary leadership generating breakthrough ideas
three_part_structure.what_it_really_is: Every person around you has 200+ valuable ideas per year - you just need to pay them immediately (even tiny amounts) and share the profits
three_part_structure.why_nothing_worked: Previous approaches were expensive and complex, focused on extracting ideas top-down rather than creating simple micro-incentives that make EVERYONE an innovator

root_cause_architecture.villain: $200,000-a-year management consultants and complex innovation programs

notable_patterns: Three ascending examples ($10, $2, $0) demonstrate universal applicability
200 ideas per year statistic creates massive value perception
Testimonials from diverse fields prove cross-industry application
genius_elements: Tiny payment amounts ($2, $10) make system feel accessible and non-threatening
Profit-sharing outcome demonstrated through 'small fortunes' description
ROI proof: $10 investment → hundreds of thousands saved
Simple to learn (few minutes), simple to set up (hour or two)
